leadText: Bug Buzz (Japanese: むしのさざめき Insect Noise) is a damage-dealing Bug-type move introduced in Generation IV. It is: TR61 in Pokémon Sword and Shield. TM62 in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl. TM162 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation IV-V 1.2 Generation VI onwards 1.3 Generation VIII 1.3.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.4 Generation IX 1.4.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Bug Buzz does damage and has a 10% chance of lowering the target's Special Defense by one stage. Bug Buzz is a sound-based move . Generation IV-V [ edit | edit source ] If used on a Pokémon behind a substitute , it hits that Pokémon's substitute. Generation VI onwards [ edit | edit source ] Bug Buzz can now hit Pokémon even if they are behind a substitute. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Bug Buzz's base power has been lowered to 80. It has a 20% chance of lowering the target's defensive stats for three turns. sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD T D S Inflicts damage on enemies within a 1-tile range. It may also reduce their Special Defense by 1 level. B S L まわりの てきポケモンに ダメージをあたえる しかも いっていのかくりつで とくぼうを １だんかいさげる MD GtI It damages nearby enemies. It could also lower their Special Defense. S MD MD RT DX It damages nearby enemies. It could also lower their Sp. Def.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Bug Buzz is the only Bug-type sound-based move . Despite the descriptions of this move stating that the usage of wings is required, Pokémon that lack wings can still learn this attack. However, in Generation IV , when this move was first introduced, all Pokémon that could learn this move did have wings.
